agent_output:
```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical cryptographic weaknesses make these API tokens trivially forgeable and vulnerable to timing attacks."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "MD5 used for security-critical token generation",
      "risk": "MD5 is cryptographically broken — known collision attacks exist. For a B2B partner token that lives 30 days, an attacker with any leaked token/timestamp pair can feasibly brute-force the secretKey or forge tokens. HMAC-SHA256 is the industry minimum for this use case.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken() and validatePartnerApiToken() — crypto.createHash('md5')",
      "reproduction": "Capture any valid token + its timestamp. MD5's speed (~4 billion hashes/sec on commodity GPU) makes offline brute-force of short or predictable secretKeys trivial. Even with strong keys, MD5 collisions allow forging alternate valid payloads.",
      "fix": "Replace with HMAC-SHA256: `crypto.createHmac('sha256', secretKey).update(payload).digest('hex')`. This provides both a proper keyed MAC and a collision-resistant hash. Do NOT concatenate the key into the message — that is susceptible to length-extension attacks.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Secure token generation for B2B partner integrations"
    },
    {
      "title": "Timing-attack vulnerable string comparison leaks token validity",
      "risk": "The `===` comparison in validatePartnerApiToken returns early on the first mismatched character. An attacker can measure response times to progressively guess each character of a valid token, fully reconstructing it without knowing the secret.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "validatePartnerApiToken() — `return token === expectedToken`",
      "reproduction": "Send validation requests varying one character at a time. Measure response latency. A correct character takes marginally longer. Over thousands of requests (feasible against a 30-day token), the full token is recovered byte-by-byte.",
      "fix": "Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(token, 'hex'), Buffer.from(expectedToken, 'hex'))`. Wrap in a length check first since timingSafeEqual throws on mismatched lengths.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Secure token validation for B2B partner integrations"
    },
    {
      "title": "No token expiry enforcement — 30-day lifetime is unenforced",
      "risk": "The token embeds `Date.now()` at generation but validatePartnerApiToken accepts any arbitrary timestamp from the caller. There is zero expiry check. A leaked or stolen token is valid forever. For B2B integrations handling courier/logistics data, this is a compliance and breach-response nightmare — you cannot revoke or expire tokens.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "validatePartnerApiToken() — timestamp parameter is caller-controlled with no bounds check",
      "reproduction": "Generate a token today. Call validatePartnerApiToken with the original timestamp one year from now. It passes. There is no window check, no expiry, no revocation path.",
      "fix": "Enforce expiry inside validation: `const age = Date.now() - timestamp; if (age < 0 || age > 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) return false;`. Also embed the expiry in the token payload itself (not just rely on caller-supplied timestamp) so the validator doesn't need external state to reject expired tokens.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Token lifecycle management — 30-day expiry requirement"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "Secret key concatenated into payload — vulnerable to length-extension attacks",
      "description": "Appending the secret to the message (`payload + secretKey`) instead of using HMAC means an attacker who knows a valid hash can compute valid hashes for extended payloads without knowing the key. This is a well-known attack against `H(message || key)` constructions. Switching to HMAC eliminates this entirely.",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken() and validatePartnerApiToken()"
    },
    {
      "title": "Generated token has no structure — timestamp not recoverable from token",
      "description": "The validator requires the caller to supply the timestamp separately. This means the system must store timestamp-to-token mappings externally. If that store is lost or the caller passes the wrong timestamp, valid tokens fail validation. The token should be self-contained (e.g., `base64(payload).signature`) so the validator can extract the timestamp from the token itself.",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken() return value"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on partnerId or secretKey",
      "description": "Empty string, undefined coerced to 'undefined', or partnerId containing ':' would produce ambiguous or colliding payloads. `partner:A` with timestamp `123` collides with `partner` and timestamp `A:123`. Add validation and use a non-ambiguous separator or structured encoding (JSON).",
      "location": "generatePartnerApiToken() parameters"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Consider using a standard token format (JWT with HS256/RS256) instead of a custom scheme — it handles expiry, structure, and signing correctly out of the box.",
    "Add a token revocation mechanism (database blocklist or short-lived tokens + refresh) since 30-day tokens for B2B partners will inevitably need emergency revocation.",
    "Log token generation and validation attempts for audit trail — B2B partner integrations typically require this for compliance."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Any tokens generated with the current MD5 scheme must be rotated after fix deployment. Partners will need re-issued tokens, requiring a migration plan and partner communication.",
    "rollback_capability": "Rolling back after deploying HMAC-SHA256 would invalidate all newly issued tokens. Must maintain dual-validation (old MD5 + new HMAC) during migration window.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Switching hash algorithm is a breaking change — all existing tokens become invalid. Requires versioned token format or parallel validation.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics visible in this code. Token validation failures should be monitored for brute-force detection.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": true,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": true,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": [
        "Changing hash algorithm invalidates all existing partner tokens",
        "Adding expiry enforcement will reject currently-valid tokens that exceed the 30-day window",
        "Token format change (if made self-contained) changes the API contract"
      ]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Deploy behind feature flag. Support dual validation (legacy MD5 + new HMAC) for 30-day migration window. Issue new tokens to partners in parallel. Kill legacy path after all partners migrate."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "MD5 is broken for security use — this is settled cryptographic consensus",
      "Timing attack on string comparison is well-documented and exploitable",
      "Missing expiry check is directly observable in the code"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [],
    "assumptions": [
      "These tokens authenticate B2B API requests and are security-critical",
      "The 30-day lifetime mentioned in the prompt is a hard requirement",
      "No additional validation layer exists upstream that would mitigate these issues"
    ]
  }
}
```
